title: Anacheilium silvanum
intro: Anacheilium silvanum, also known as the Silvan Anacheilium or Purple Passion Orchid, is a species of orchid native to the tropical forests of Central and South America. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can aid in wound healing and the treatment of respiratory infections. The bioactive constituents of Anacheilium silvanum include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Anacheilium silvanum include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to support immune function and alleviate symptoms of fever and colds.
taxonomy: Anacheilium silvanum is classified within the family Orchidaceae, commonly known as the orchid family. This family comprises of approximately 30,000 known species of orchids, making it the second-largest family of flowering plants. Anacheilium silvanum, a species of orchid, is further grouped into the subfamily Epidendroideae. The subfamily Epidendroideae is one of the six main subfamilies within the Orchidaceae family.
